mission.title: Install and Configure CloudNativePG (PostgreSQL Operator)
mission.description: CloudNativePG is an open-source PostgreSQL operator designed for Kubernetes, enabling seamless management of PostgreSQL databases throughout their lifecycle. It automates database operations using Kubernetes-native principles, making it suitable for production environments where high availability and scalability are essential.

steps.title: Add the CloudNativePG Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the official CloudNativePG Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration, allowing you to install the operator easily.

```bash

helm repo add cloudnative-pg https://charts.cloudnative-pg.io

```

> This command adds the repository where the CloudNativePG charts are hosted.

steps.title: Install the CloudNativePG operator
steps.description:
This command installs the CloudNativePG operator in the 'cloudnative-pg' namespace, which is created automatically if it doesn't exist.

```bash

helm install cloudnative-pg cloudnative-pg/cloudnative-pg --namespace cloudnative-pg --create-namespace --version 1.28.1

```

> Specifying the version ensures you are using the latest stable release.

steps.title: Create a PostgreSQL Cluster Custom Resource
steps.description:
This step creates a PostgreSQL cluster using a Custom Resource (CR) definition, which defines the desired state of your PostgreSQL deployment.

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: postgres.cloudnative-pg.io/v1
kind: Cluster
metadata:
  name: my-postgres-cluster
  namespace: cloudnative-pg
spec:
  instances: 3
  postgresql:
    version: '15'
EOF

```

> This YAML defines a cluster with 3 instances of PostgreSQL version 15.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: postgres.cloudnative-pg.io/v1
kind: Cluster
metadata:
  name: my-postgres-cluster
  namespace: cloudnative-pg
spec:
  instances: 3
  postgresql:
    version: '15'
EOF

steps.title: Verify the PostgreSQL Cluster is running
steps.description:
This command checks the status of the PostgreSQL cluster to ensure it has been created and is running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get clusters -n cloudnative-pg

```

> You should see 'my-postgres-cluster' listed with a status of 'Ready'.

uninstall.title: Uninstall the CloudNativePG operator
uninstall.description:
This step removes the CloudNativePG operator and its associated resources. Be aware that this will delete the PostgreSQL cluster and all its data.

```bash

helm uninstall cloudnative-pg --namespace cloudnative-pg

kubectl delete namespace cloudnative-pg

```

> This command will remove the operator and all resources created by it.
> This command deletes the namespace, which also removes all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade the CloudNativePG operator
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you have a backup of your PostgreSQL data. This step will upgrade the operator and verify its status afterward.

```bash

kubectl get clusters -n cloudnative-pg -o yaml > backup-cluster.yaml

helm upgrade cloudnative-pg cloudnative-pg/cloudnative-pg --namespace cloudnative-pg --version 1.28.1

kubectl get clusters -n cloudnative-pg

```

> Backup the current cluster configuration.
> This command upgrades the operator to the specified version.
> Verify that the cluster is still running after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get clusters -n cloudnative-pg -o yaml > backup-cluster.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to create cluster: ...'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This error may occur if the required permissions are not set for the operator.

**Fix:**
Ensure that the operator has the necessary RBAC permissions to create resources in the specified namespace.

troubleshooting.title: Cluster status shows 'Pending'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may indicate insufficient resources in the cluster to schedule the PostgreSQL instances.

**Fix:**
Check the resource requests and limits in your Cluster CR and ensure your nodes have enough resources.

troubleshooting.title: PostgreSQL instances are not starting
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This can happen if there are configuration errors in the Custom Resource.

**Fix:**
Review the logs of the PostgreSQL pods using 'kubectl logs <pod-name>' to identify configuration issues.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Connection refused' when accessing PostgreSQL
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may be due to network policies or service misconfigurations.

**Fix:**
Check the service configuration and ensure that the network policies allow traffic to the PostgreSQL service.

versionNotes.changes: Improved support for PostgreSQL 15, added new metrics for monitoring, and enhanced backup capabilities.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see the PostgreSQL cluster listed with 'my-postgres-cluster' and a status of 'Ready' when running 'kubectl get clusters -n cloudnative-pg'.

EOF

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running version 1.26 or higher and the necessary tools installed.
